figures.caption: Table 2: Core behavioural measures by agent and schedule. Post-reversal regret is computed from known reward probabilities over a 20-trial window following each true state switch and is reported in expected coins (higher = worse).

Fig. 2: Posterior group-level parameter densities under Dual RL and Dual RLκDU (left vs. right rows) for fixed and random schedules (bottom vs. top rows). Panels show gain and loss learning rates (ηpos, ηneg), inverse temperature (β), and (for κDU) the counterfactual updating parameter (κ).
Table 3: Hierarchical posterior group-level estimates of computational parameters (mean (SD)).
Fig. 1: Random schedule: post-reversal adoption of the target option and eventaligned choice proportions around reversals.
